category_title: Adolescent student with blindness and selective mutism who uses voice-output devices for communication
minimal_context: The student is answering yes/no listening comprehension questions using a voice-output device after reading a Braille text.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We finished the story. Here is a listening comprehension question with two choices on your voice-output device. Does the person in this story eat the gumdrops right away?

STUDENT: (Keeps the fingertips of both hands on the Braille lines of the story she just listened to.)

TUTOR: Please answer the question.

STUDENT: (Remains silent when given the opportunity to respond to the listening comprehension question.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and show me your answer.

STUDENT: (Independently places her hand on the communicator and then hesitates.)

options.A: Change the M&M reinforcers provided during the listening comprehension activity on an almost daily basis throughout the course of the one-on-one instruction.
options.B: Guide the student's fingertips over the Braille letters on the voice-output device while reading the possible responses aloud, and then return her hand to a neutral position to allow her to select a response.
options.C: Assign listening comprehension questions that the student has already mastered, as opposed to novel questions, during this portion of the one-on-one instruction session.
options.D: Allow the student to withdraw from the instructional environment for a time-limited period of quiet time to prevent an emotional overload before returning to the device.
